Ok girls, face it no bra is a 5 star rating just for the fact that no one wants to wear a bra!!! But if you have to wear one, then this is a 5 star review bra. I would never have thought I'd put pictures up, and I still hesitate in doing so, but I really feel it's important because I know I cant be the only one having so many problems finding a good fitting bra!!!! I spent hours and hours looking for a white, no wire, seamless bra in my size, and to find one in my price range of under $50. This was the only one I found that I knew and trusted. It also takes hours because I really do look at other reviewers. I start by one star reviews and work my way up. I also look at who the reviewers are, for instance, one bra I looked at had a lot of great reviews but when I looked at the reviews, they were really off grammatically. One was a guy who reviewed his G Cup bra (I know there can be transgender reviewers, but not usually ones who said they bought a G Cup bra). I see many Chinese companies who, if they don't have good reviews on a product, will put new listings up and you will see maybe 5 or 6 reviews and they are almost all the same.

At this size I've only worn Bali or Glamorise so I've always liked Glamorise and was surprised to see the negative reviews. Many reviewers said that the band seemed too small. One thing I wonder is if they are measuring correctly

top_rant.text: Pleated cheer skirts seem to have fallen out of favor and These box-pleated cheer skirts with the contrast-color insets appear to be unpopular if not particularly hard to find. The cheerleading supply houses tend to have a minimum order & the online secondhand marketplaces are a crap shoot so I was lucky to find one at a reasonable price in the correct color-combo (Columbia blue & white) to match an 80s cheer sweater I got for a screaming deal on eBay. The more pleats, the splashier they look; This one has 8 pleats- the bare minimum for a legit cheerleader look & the minimum number of pleats in this type of skirt. 16 is the maximum but I think its overkill. 10 or 12 pleats is optimal, IMO but you take what you can get. My waist measures 30.5” & my hips are 42”, I’m 5’5” & my dress size is a 10 or 12- which isn’t great because for Letter-size garments a 10 is paired with an 8(which is too small) and a 12 is paired to a 14(which is too large. So, naturally I fell between sizes for this too.  I ordered an extra-large but I should have ordered a large. That was the sizing recommendation Amazon gave me but historically, those haven’t been very reliable- especially with shoes & when shopping for men’s/unisex tops for myself. Yeesh. First off I am not your regular jacket user. I will get into that in a minute.
Next a few observations about this jacket :
* Over all it is quite well made.

* My boss who NEVER ever hands out a complement or voices much of an opinion on anything good . . . said on first laying eyes on my all black one and I quote "NICE JACKET" !.

* There is no second zipper pull to zip up from the bottom; handy if you want to cinch up a tool belt a notch tighter etc.

* The sleeves are long enough for the size; many times I find jacket sleeves a bit short for really active use.

* THE SLEEVES ARE LINED WITH SLICKish fabric; not the micro fleece. Most people will want this for easy on off over other clothes (I was hoping for micro fleece in the sleeves but as I said I am abnormal : ).

* The sleeves have a thin but very effective layer of synthetic insulation in the arms. Just the right amount for street wear; maybe too much (preferably none) for bicycle commuting.

* The hood is more than an after thought; it is double layer and well made. Works well for street use. Comes forward too much for cycling (I use a pro neoprene balaclava with on off nose cover).

I am very happy with this little "Fleiger style" watch, particularly with it's dial, which compares to any that I have seen in the affordable watch space. The dial has a great deal of well composed detail, this is subtle and hard to see in photographs. In person this very inexpensive watch actually looks high-end.

It is indeed a small watch, 36.5 mm; be aware of that. It's cute as a button, and not much bigger. I have to adjust a bit after wearing this watch to a feeling that my other watches are clownishly large.

Of the 5 different models in the SNK80x series, I recommend this one. This model has a different "color scheme", not just a different colored dial. Notice the black outlining of the handset (the others all have monochromatic handsets). The slender ring between the edge and center of the watch, providing separation between the hours and minute numerals, is gold on this model, but black or white on the other models. The lighter dial provides a background that allows similar adjacent colors of the silver Seiko logo and golden '5" numeral to appear more distinctly, and this cream- light-beige tone also nicely complements the pale green color of the 'super-luminova' application around the dial and on the handset. These, and other little subtleties of the color scheme implemented for the SNK803, add sophistication to the d

I just LOVE these divers from Invicta! I have a number of them, but, until now, did not have one in this design, not only in the all black, but with the "open heart" movement, so, in looking to get a simple black and silver automatic diver, I was very pleased to come across this one. This kind of finishes out my Invicta diver collection (although I do have one more on my list), and it's beautiful piece. Although listed as a "Pro Diver" this model is actually a "Grand Diver," it should be noted.

For those considering the brand, please don't be swayed by erroneous complaints and negativity. It is a solid brand, and especially in model lines such as this diver series, you're going to get a great watch. I began, in watch collecting, with Invicta, and I don't collect to sell or trade, I collect watches because I love them and, after buying just a few, many long years ago, it became a bit of a hobby and passion to learn what makes a good watch and what makes one worth buying. I'll always be thankful to this brand for launching me into a real love of watches.

This is a colorful hoodie for young girls. This is an XL - or as stated under the XL size, it says 12 years. My grand girl is 11 and wears a size 14 shirt and this DOES NOT fit her. My big concern is that it is 100% cotton -- so we will wash in cold and LINE DRY. I would not dare throw this hoodie in the dryer even on a low setting.

Size issues aside, this is a decent LOOKING little hoodie. It is bright and colorful; the yellow and white designs against the royal blue of the hoodie make for a bright and eye-catching hoodie that looks great with jeans. top_five_star_obscene.text: So many knock offs of crocs that it's easy to confuse them for the real thing. I've picked up the fake ones, even though I've had the real ones. But maybe your old ones wore out and you're at the everything for $5 store and you buy the crappy ones, and you just end up forgetting how great and different real crocs are. If you need extra room in the toe area to use toe separators, or you have wide feet, and/or you have some foot issues with pain, CROCS can be a saver for your feet. When I put mine on today, after having worn the crappy ones for a while, I was excited to have them again as I felt the little nubs inside stimulate my feet. They will help your circulation. I just love them!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I previously bought a pair of these jeans to replace a retired pair of my Arizona jeans, but they arrived in the wrong size and therefore became capris (which I liked). I decided on getting another pair, but this time chose the "long" length and do not regret it. I am 5'6" and around 140 lbs, and I bought the size 4 which fit me perfectly. The legs are indeed very long, which I anticipate will slowly shrink over the course of many washes down to the perfect length. For now, I just cuff them at the bottom and they're cute and functional. I will likely wear these with some boots most of the winter.
My only qualm with these jeans is that it seems Levi's are becoming more and more cheaply made as the years pass. My first pair were thick, sturdy, and lasted about 3 years; I predict maybe getting a year out of these tops. They seem very thin, not protective at all against brush when I go hiking this fall. The stitching seems solid, only a couple very small errors which shouldn't give me any grief. Amazing shirt! I have no complaint. I don't know what S, M, L means in dress shirts as I'm a 16-17 in dress shirts in US sizes. However, I went through many reviews and tried to find reviews that mention sizes, specifically chest size in US measurements. Many said to get a size up, that is gibberish to me, as what works for one does not work for all. However, I took a gamble and was not disappointed. The large worked for me with room to spare. I', 5'8" 180# chest 38" for a suit jacket pants 32-34" depending on the make! It's totally complicated to shop nowadays as most companies are doing too much extra crap with their design... skinny, slim fit... etc! I don't care for that extra stuff I just want normal-fitting pants and shirts. is that too much to ask for? ugh! and I'm only 38 years old and still struggling with all this slim crap they have all over!!!!!!

hope my review helps the normal folks that just want a normal-fitting shirt and pants!
